title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Repository Trace: Repository Triage
summary: Recover and ship a repository containing mixed work, a conflict, a mistaken commit, a remote update, and a release deadline. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git repository triage increment in a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.
Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Repository Triage
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Repository Triage contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.

content.text: A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Repository Triage condition decided your prediction.

content.value: gitrevisions Manual — Controls revision names, ancestry, range, exclusion, reflog, and object selection semantics.

steps.title: Bound Repository Triage
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v3.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Repository Triage application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.

content.text: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree. A defensible solution applies Repository Triage, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 2
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: git status alone captures all state needed for repository recovery. This fails the Repository Triage contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Repository Triage evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-repository-triage-170jmd" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Repository Triage into observable performance evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

A teammate acts on this belief: “git status alone captures all state needed for repository recovery.” Which repair addresses the cause?

steps.title: Defend the Repository Triage model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Repository Triage: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v3.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.
